evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to this shared IdScannerScreenModel to scan passports, driver's licenses, and ID cards from any region. I need data from both sides of the document. When a document is recognized, show the full name and document number. When a document is rejected, show an appropriate message.
evals.expected_output: IdScannerScreenModel implementing IdCaptureListener with a shared DataCaptureContext, IdCaptureSettings built via IdCaptureSettings.idCaptureSettings() with acceptedDocuments for IdCard, DriverLicense and Passport, scannerType set to FullDocumentScanner(), IdCapture.forContext(...), a DataCaptureView + IdCaptureOverlay set up in setupDataCaptureView, and onIdCaptured/onIdRejected handling.

assertions.text: Imports from com.kmp.datacapture.id.* and com.kmp.datacapture.core.* — does NOT import com.scandit.datacapture.* (native Android/iOS namespace)
Creates settings with IdCaptureSettings.idCaptureSettings() — does NOT use a bare IdCaptureSettings() constructor
Sets acceptedDocuments to include IdCard(IdCaptureRegion.ANY), DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.ANY), and Passport(IdCaptureRegion.ANY)
Sets settings.scannerType = FullDocumentScanner() — does NOT wrap it in an IdCaptureScanner(...) constructor and does NOT use SingleSideScanner
Creates the mode with IdCapture.forContext(dataCaptureContext, settings) — does NOT use IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext (native Android) or an IdCapture(context:settings:) initializer (iOS)
Uses Camera.getDefaultCamera(IdCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) and calls dataCaptureContext.setFrameSource(...) with it
Creates the overlay with IdCaptureOverlay.withIdCapture(idCapture) or IdCaptureOverlay.withIdCaptureForView(idCapture, view) — does NOT use IdCaptureOverlay.newInstance(...)
Implements IdCaptureListener with onIdCaptured(mode: IdCapture, id: CapturedId) and onIdRejected(mode: IdCapture, id: CapturedId?, reason: RejectionReason)
Sets mode.isEnabled = false at the top of onIdCaptured and onIdRejected
Reads capturedId.fullName and capturedId.documentNumber from the CapturedId
Registers the listener once via addListener when the mode is created, not repeatedly per lifecycle event
Does NOT reference AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier

evals.prompt: Update the ID Capture integration to reject expired IDs and holders under 21 years old. Show a distinct message for each rejection reason: expired document, underage holder, timeout, and any other rejection.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings updated with rejectExpiredIds = true and rejectHolderBelowAge = 21, and onIdRejected updated with a when on RejectionReason showing distinct messages for DOCUMENT_EXPIRED, HOLDER_UNDERAGE, TIMEOUT, and an else branch.

assertions.text: Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
Sets settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 21 as an Int literal, not a String
Uses a when statement on the RejectionReason parameter in onIdRejected
Has a branch for RejectionReason.DOCUMENT_EXPIRED with a distinct message about the document being expired
Has a branch for RejectionReason.HOLDER_UNDERAGE with a distinct message about the age requirement
Has a branch for RejectionReason.TIMEOUT with a distinct message about the capture timing out
Has an else branch for all other rejection reasons

evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to accept ID cards and driver's licenses from the US, but explicitly reject passports.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing IdCard(IdCaptureRegion.US) and DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US), and rejectedDocuments containing Passport(IdCaptureRegion.ANY).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to listOf(IdCard(IdCaptureRegion.US), DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US)) — does NOT put passports in acceptedDocuments
Sets rejectedDocuments to contain Passport — does NOT try to exclude passports via acceptedDocuments alone
Builds settings via IdCaptureSettings.idCaptureSettings()

evals.prompt: I only need to read the barcode from US driver's licenses — I don't need the VIZ or MRZ.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US) and scannerType set directly to SingleSideScanner(barcode = true, machineReadableZone = false, visualInspectionZone = false).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to listOf(DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US))
Sets settings.scannerType = SingleSideScanner(barcode = true, machineReadableZone = false, visualInspectionZone = false) — does NOT use FullDocumentScanner
Assigns the SingleSideScanner directly to scannerType — does NOT wrap it in an IdCaptureScanner(...) constructor

evals.prompt: I only need to read the MRZ from passports — I don't need the VIZ or barcode.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing Passport and scannerType set directly to SingleSideScanner(barcode = false, machineReadableZone = true, visualInspectionZone = false).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport
Sets settings.scannerType = SingleSideScanner(barcode = false, machineReadableZone = true, visualInspectionZone = false) — does NOT use FullDocumentScanner
Assigns the SingleSideScanner directly to scannerType — does NOT wrap it in an IdCaptureScanner(...) constructor

evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to scan US driver's licenses only and verify them for forged AAMVA barcodes and data inconsistency between the front and back of the document. Also reject expired IDs. On rejection for inconsistent data, extract and display the front review image from the verification result.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings targeting DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US) with rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes, rejectInconsistentData, and rejectExpiredIds flags, setShouldPassImageTypeToResult for CROPPED_DOCUMENT, plus a note that the id-aamva-barcode-verification Gradle/SPM add-on dependency is required, and correct VerificationResult access in onIdRejected.

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to listOf(DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.US)) only
Sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true
Sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true
Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
Calls settings.setShouldPassImageTypeToResult(IdImageType.CROPPED_DOCUMENT, true) — required for frontReviewImage to be populated
Does NOT use a standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier class — verification is settings-driven (semantic)
In onIdRejected, accesses id?.verificationResult?.dataConsistency?.frontReviewImage when the reason is RejectionReason.INCONSISTENT_DATA
Handles RejectionReason.INCONSISTENT_DATA, RejectionReason.FORGED_AAMVA_BARCODE, and RejectionReason.DOCUMENT_EXPIRED in onIdRejected with distinct messages
Mentions or implies that the id-aamva-barcode-verification Gradle/SPM dependency must be added alongside id for the settings flags to take effect at runtime (semantic)

evals.prompt: Configure this ID Capture integration so that sensitive fields are redacted AND obscured with black boxes on the returned document images, not just removed from the data.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with anonymizationMode set to IdAnonymizationMode.FIELDS_AND_IMAGES so that anonymized fields are both nulled in the CapturedId and covered on the returned images.

assertions.text: Sets settings.anonymizationMode = IdAnonymizationMode.FIELDS_AND_IMAGES
Uses the IdAnonymizationMode enum value FIELDS_AND_IMAGES — does NOT use a string literal
Does NOT set anonymizationMode to NONE, FIELDS_ONLY, or IMAGES_ONLY (semantic)

evals.prompt: Update ID Capture to reject voided documents (for example a hole-punched or VOID-stamped US driver's license) and show a distinct message when that happens. Also make sure I've added the right dependency for this to actually work.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with rejectVoidedIds = true, an onIdRejected when that handles RejectionReason.DOCUMENT_VOIDED with a distinct message, and a note that the id-voided-detection Gradle/SPM add-on dependency is required alongside id.

assertions.text: Sets settings.rejectVoidedIds = true
Handles the RejectionReason.DOCUMENT_VOIDED case in onIdRejected with a distinct voided-document message
Does NOT use a standalone verifier class for voided detection — rejection is settings-driven (semantic)
Mentions that the id-voided-detection Gradle/SPM dependency must be added for the flag to take effect at runtime (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need the vehicle category data from the back of European driving licenses. Enable extraction of that data, and in onIdCaptured iterate over the driving license categories and log each category's code.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with decodeBackOfEuropeanDrivingLicense = true, and onIdCaptured reading capturedId.viz?.drivingLicenseDetails?.drivingLicenseCategories and accessing each category's .code, plus a note that the id-europe-driving-license add-on dependency is required.

assertions.text: Sets settings.decodeBackOfEuropeanDrivingLicense = true
Reads id.viz?.drivingLicenseDetails?.drivingLicenseCategories in onIdCaptured — NOT id.vizResult
Accesses the category code as .code on each category — does NOT use .categoryCode
Mentions that the id-europe-driving-license Gradle/SPM dependency must be added for the flag to take effect at runtime (semantic)

evals.prompt: I need to scan mobile driver's licenses (mDL, ISO 18013-5) and read the holder's name and date of birth. Set that up in IntegratedScreenModel.kt.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings whose scannerType is set directly to MobileDocumentScanner(iso180135 = true, ...), and onIdCaptured reading capturedId.mobileDocument (or the harmonized top-level fields).

assertions.text: Sets settings.scannerType = MobileDocumentScanner(iso180135 = true, ...) (semantic)
Assigns the MobileDocumentScanner directly to scannerType — does NOT wrap it in an IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument:, mobileDocument:) combined constructor, which does not exist on KMP (semantic)
onIdCaptured reads id.mobileDocument, or the harmonized top-level fields the mobile document populates (semantic)
Does NOT invent a separate add-on dependency for mobile documents — MobileDocumentScanner is in the base id module (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to build the ID scanning screen using Jetpack Compose Multiplatform instead of hosting a DataCaptureView manually. Set it up to scan any ID card, driver's license, or passport, showing a rounded overlay, and log the captured document's full name.
evals.expected_output: A @Composable function using IdCaptureView from the id-compose module with settings from IdCaptureSettings.idCaptureSettings(), overlayStyle = IdLayoutStyle.ROUNDED, and an onCapture callback reading capturedId.fullName, plus the core-compose and id-compose Gradle dependencies.

assertions.text: Uses the @Composable IdCaptureView function from com.kmp.datacapture.id.compose
Passes settings = IdCaptureSettings.idCaptureSettings() to IdCaptureView
Sets overlayStyle = IdLayoutStyle.ROUNDED
Implements onCapture to read the captured document's fullName
Mentions adding both the core-compose and id-compose Gradle/SPM dependencies (semantic)
Does NOT manually construct a DataCaptureView or IdCaptureOverlay for this composable-based setup (semantic)
